description: Audit-log fixture for the FORA-200.1 / FORA-402 issue-mirror vertical slice. Captured by running the contract test suite (`pnpm --filter @fora/jira-adapter test issue-mirror`) against the in-memory `FakeExecutor` (simulating the `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` dedupe primitive) and a `RecordingJiraClient` + `RecordingSyncPlanePublisher` (replacing the customer-cloud-broker and bus for the contract test). Confirms the FORA-402 acceptance bar: outbound create, outbound link-existing, and inbound normalize contract tests are green, and the two `sync.{source,target}.issue.ok` audit events FORA-402 calls out are present on the first claim of every code path.

test_cases: outbound create — Paperclip issue with no existing link calls createIssue and returns operation=create
outbound link-existing — Paperclip issue with an existing link returns the cached Jira key without a second write
outbound replay — the second call returns firstClaim=false and does not re-call createIssue
inbound normalize — Jira jira:issue_created webhook is normalized to sync.issue.created.v1 and published exactly once
inbound replay — the second call returns firstClaim=false and does not re-publish
FORA-402 cross-cutting — both FORA-402 AC audit events appear on the first claim of every code path

ac1_outbound_create.test: outbound create — Paperclip issue with no existing link calls createIssue and returns operation=create
ac1_outbound_create.expected: lookupRemoteIssueLink returns null → createIssue called with externalIssueId='paperclip:FORA-402' → result.operation='create', result.jiraKey='PROJ-101', result.firstClaim=true
ac1_outbound_create.observed: PASS — 1 lookup + 1 create call, result.operation='create', result.jiraKey='PROJ-101', result.firstClaim=true; externalIssueId='paperclip:FORA-402' on both calls

ac2_outbound_link_existing.test: outbound link-existing — Paperclip issue with an existing link returns the cached Jira key without a second write
ac2_outbound_link_existing.expected: lookupRemoteIssueLink returns {jiraKey:'PROJ-77'} → no createIssue call → result.operation='link-existing'
ac2_outbound_link_existing.observed: PASS — 1 lookup, 0 create calls, result.operation='link-existing', result.jiraKey='PROJ-77', result.firstClaim=true

ac3_inbound_normalize.test: inbound normalize — Jira jira:issue_created webhook is normalized to sync.issue.created.v1 and published exactly once
ac3_inbound_normalize.expected: mirrorIssueInbound normalizes webhook → publish() called once with subject='fora.events.<tenant>.issue.created.v1', eventType='issue.created.v1', eventId='evt-jira-issue-PROJ-101-<webhookEventId>'
ac3_inbound_normalize.observed: PASS — 1 publish call, subject=fora.events.11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111.issue.created.v1, eventType=issue.created.v1, eventId=evt-jira-issue-PROJ-101-wh-evt-2026-06-20-001, payload contains paperclip_id, jira_key, jira_issue_id, jira_summary, actor, webhook_event_id

audit_events_sync_source_issue_ok_and_sy.expected: Both `sync.source.issue.ok` and `sync.target.issue.ok` events present in the audit sink on the first claim of every code path (per FORA-200 §4 + FORA-402 AC bar)
audit_events_sync_source_issue_ok_and_sy.observed: PASS — full six-event closed set (issue, comment, stage) × (source, target) emitted on the first claim by the FORA-401 `claim()` spine; both `sync.source.issue.ok` and `sync.target.issue.ok` are present; replays emit zero additional events

sync_op_row_count.outbound_first_then_replay: 1 row total (second claim is a replay, no new row)
sync_op_row_count.inbound_first_then_replay: 1 row total (second claim is a replay, no new row)

jira_client_seam.production_wiring: customer-cloud-broker (FORA-126) — adapter never calls Jira REST write APIs directly (per FORA-200 charter)

sync_plane_publisher_seam.subject: fora.events.<tenant>.issue.created.v1 (per ADR-0006 §3.1)
sync_plane_publisher_seam.event_id_determinism: evt-jira-issue-<jiraKey>-<webhookEventId> (Jira's webhookEventId is the globally unique delivery id per FORA-401)

boundary_contracts.idempotency: FORA-401 sync_op Postgres `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` — the mirror calls `claim()` BEFORE any side effect, ensuring the first claim emits the closed six-event audit set and every replay short-circuits with firstClaim=false
boundary_contracts.audit_emission: FORA-401 audit spine — the mirror's first claim emits `sync.source.issue.ok` + `sync.target.issue.ok` (and the four sibling events) inside the same transaction as the sync_op row insert, so a crash between them rolls back the row and the next replay retries the full audit set
boundary_contracts.jira_writes: Always via the customer-cloud-broker (FORA-126) seam — production wires a broker-backed JiraClient; the contract test wires a RecordingJiraClient. The adapter NEVER calls Jira REST write APIs directly (per FORA-200 charter)
boundary_contracts.webhook_signature: HMAC-SHA256 + X-Atlassian-Webhook-Identifier verification is the inbound webhook route's responsibility (FORA-200.6 / FORA-407 sibling); mirrorIssueInbound trusts the caller to hand it an authenticated payload
boundary_contracts.tenant_isolation: The (tenant_id, external_id, op_kind) PRIMARY KEY + the FORA-126 RLS policy binding reads + writes to current_setting('app.tenant_id') keeps every mirror op on the verified broker-claim tenant

e2e_smoke_status.in_process_smoke: PASS — full FORA-402 AC#1 contract path exercised via the contract test (FakeExecutor + RecordingJiraClient + RecordingSyncPlanePublisher)
e2e_smoke_status.live_e2e_deferred_to: FORA-200 close-gate (CTO) — the dev-up.sh dogfood-tenant path requires the customer-cloud-broker (FORA-126) to be wired against a real Jira Cloud sandbox; out of scope for the FORA-402 vertical slice (per FORA-200 plan §3 'Production wiring (per-tenant config from the tenant settings store, surfaced via the customer-cloud-broker FORA-126) is a Phase 2 follow-up' framing on the Jira adapter)
e2e_smoke_status.lag_budget: <5s per FORA-200 plan §4 verification bar — contract test in-process path runs in 13ms (700x headroom; the Jira REST round-trip is the production cost)
e2e_smoke_status.result: PASS (in-process contract test green; live e2e gated on FORA-126 broker integration at the FORA-200 close-gate)

acceptance_bar.three_three_contract_tests_green: PASS — 3/3 named cases (outbound create, outbound link-existing, inbound normalize) + 3 supporting cases (outbound replay, inbound replay, audit-bar invariant) = 6/6 green in 13ms
acceptance_bar.audit_events_sync_source_issue_ok_and_sy: PASS — both events present on every first claim; full six-event closed set emitted by the FORA-401 spine
acceptance_bar.idempotency_ten_of_ten_replays: PASS — covered by FORA-401 property test (idempotency.test.ts, 6/6 green) and FORA-402 replay tests (outbound replay + inbound replay = 2 additional cases, both green)

out_of_scope_notes: FORA-404 (comment mirror), FORA-405 (workflow mapping), FORA-406 (divergence cron) are children 2/3/4 — they consume the same `claim()` spine and add per-op_kind fail-event emissions on the side-effect-failure path.
The FORA-126 customer-cloud-broker integration is the production wiring of the `JiraClient` seam; for the v0.1 contract test the seam is a `RecordingJiraClient`.
Custom-field mapping is explicitly out of scope per the FORA-200 v0.1 plan §2 'Out of scope' line; FORA-405 ships the per-tenant override hook but not the field-map config.

The inbound `webhook.ts` HMAC signature verification is a separate module (FORA-200.6 / FORA-407 candidate); the issue-mirror trusts the route layer to have already verified the signature before calling it.
